comment_text.sn35.65:1.5: This phrasing is distinctive of this series of discourses (up to SN 35.68).

root_text.sn35.65:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn35.65:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā samiddhi yena bhagavā …pe…
root_text.sn35.65:1.5: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, māro vā assa mārapaññatti vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.65:2.1: “Yattha kho, samiddhi, atthi cakkhu, atthi rūpā, atthi cakkhuviññāṇaṁ, atthi cakkhuviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:2.2: Atthi sotaṁ, atthi saddā, atthi sotaviññāṇaṁ, atthi sotaviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:2.3: Atthi ghānaṁ, atthi gandhā, atthi ghānaviññāṇaṁ, atthi ghānaviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:2.4: Atthi jivhā, atthi rasā, atthi jivhāviññāṇaṁ, atthi jivhāviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:2.5: Atthi kāyo, atthi phoṭṭhabbā, atthi kāyaviññāṇaṁ, atthi kāyaviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:2.6: Atthi mano, atthi dhammā, atthi manoviññāṇaṁ, atthi manoviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, atthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:3.1: Yattha ca kho, samiddhi, natthi cakkhu, natthi rūpā, natthi cakkhuviññāṇaṁ, natthi cakkhuviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, natthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:3.4: natthi jivhā, natthi rasā, natthi jivhāviññāṇaṁ, natthi jivhāviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, natthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā.
root_text.sn35.65:3.6: Natthi mano, natthi dhammā, natthi manoviññāṇaṁ, natthi manoviññāṇaviññātabbā dhammā, natthi tattha māro vā mārapaññatti vā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.65:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn35.65:1.2: Then Venerable Samiddhi went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.65:1.4: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘Māra’.
translation_text.sn35.65:1.5: How do we define Māra or what is known as Māra?”
translation_text.sn35.65:2.1: “Samiddhi, where there is the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and things knowable by eye consciousness, there is Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:2.2: Where there is the ear, sounds, ear consciousness, and things knowable by ear consciousness, there is Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:2.3: Where there is the nose, smells, nose consciousness, and things knowable by nose consciousness, there is Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:2.4: Where there is the tongue, tastes, tongue consciousness, and things knowable by tongue consciousness, there is Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:2.5: Where there is the body, touches, body consciousness, and things knowable by body consciousness, there is Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:2.6: Where there is the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and things knowable by mind consciousness, there is Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:3.1: Where there is no eye, no sights, no eye consciousness, and no things knowable by eye consciousness, there is no Māra or what is known as Māra.
translation_text.sn35.65:3.6: Where there is no mind, no ideas, no mind consciousness, and no things knowable by mind consciousness, there is no Māra or what is known as Māra.”
